message:
feat(host): add HTTP cache headers for discovery and introspection endpoints

Propagate cache metadata from the discovery layer into proper HTTP response
headers so that clients can leverage conditional requests (ETag/If-None-Match,
Last-Modified/If-Modified-Since) to avoid redundant data transfer.

Discovery and introspection changes:
- discover_handler now returns (HeaderMap, Json) with ETag, Last-Modified,
  Cache-Control, and Vary headers derived from CacheMetadata
- introspect_handler accepts If-None-Match/If-Modified-Since from HTTP headers
  and merges them with any request-body validators before delegating to
  introspect_with_cache
- Fix introspection cache consistency: use cached connector list instead of
  calling registry.get() directly, which could return volatile per-call fields
  (e.g. last_health_check) that defeat ETag-based 304 responses

Test coverage:
- Add VolatileGetRegistry test helper that mutates fields on each get() call
  to verify introspection uses the cached snapshot
- Add cache_validator_from_headers and merge_cache_validator unit tests
- Extend integration tests to verify ETag, Last-Modified, Cache-Control
  headers are present and correct in discover/introspect responses
- Add supply_chain_integration test suite
- Update CBOR test vectors for enrollment and simulation requests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
